title: RemediationVerification
description: Records the outcome of post-remediation verification. After a remediation action is executed, the system monitors metrics for the verification window (default 15 minutes per drift-remediation.yaml) to confirm the drift is resolved. This is an audit artifact and is immutable after creation.

verification_id.description: Unique identifier for this verification record.

remediation_id.description: Identifier of the remediation action being verified. References remediation-action.schema.json.

component.description: Component being verified. Must match the component in the remediation action record.

baseline_id.description: Baseline used for comparison during verification. References baseline.schema.json.

verification_window.description: The observation window used for verification.

duration_minutes.description: Configured verification window duration. Default is 15 minutes per drift-remediation.yaml.

started_at.description: When the verification observation window began.

ended_at.description: When the verification observation window ended.

early_termination.description: Whether verification was terminated before the full window elapsed (e.g., due to immediate re-drift or worsening metrics).

early_termination_reason.description: Reason for early termination, if applicable.

checks.description: Individual metric checks performed during the verification window.

metric_name.description: Name of the metric being checked (e.g., 'error_rate', 'latency_p99', 'config_hash').

pre_remediation_value.description: Value observed before remediation (at drift detection time).

post_remediation_value.description: Value observed after remediation (during verification window).

value.description: Threshold value (percent deviation for within_percent, absolute value for others).

result.description: Whether the post-remediation value meets the threshold. 'inconclusive' if insufficient data was collected during the window.

sampled_at.description: Timestamp of the metric sample used for this check.

result.description: Aggregate verification result. 'pass' = all checks pass, drift resolved. 'fail' = one or more critical checks failed. 'partial' = some checks pass, non-critical checks inconclusive. 'inconclusive' = insufficient data to determine.

rationale.description: Explanation of the verdict, referencing specific checks.

checks_inconclusive.description: Number of checks that were inconclusive.

revert.description: Revert action taken if verification failed. Only present when revert_on_verification_failure is true in drift-remediation.yaml and verification failed.

reverted.description: Whether the remediation action was reverted.

revert_action.description: Description of what was done to revert.

escalated_after_revert.description: Whether the issue was escalated to human review after revert.

new_baseline.description: New baseline captured after successful verification. Only present when verdict is 'pass'.

baseline_id.description: ID of the new baseline. References baseline.schema.json.

captured_at.description: When the new baseline was captured.

supersedes.description: ID of the previous baseline that this one supersedes.

created_at.description: When this verification record was created.
